zh.lifeSummary: 當Juan Garcia de Toledo於1257年出生在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多托萊多時，他的父親García Ibáñez de Toledo25歲，母親María Álvarez22歲。他和Inés García de Toledo至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1288年10月14日在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多享年31歲。

nameMeanings.name: Garcia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jesus, Carlos, Luis, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Mario, Raul, Roberto, Ramon, Rafael.  Portuguese Wenceslao, Joao, Paulo, Catarina, Albeiro, Ligia, Godofredo, Lidio, Wenseslao, Adao, Afonso.

Spanish (García) and Portuguese: from a medieval personal name of uncertain origin. It is normally found in medieval records in the Latin form Garsea, and may well be of pre-Roman origin, perhaps akin to Basque (h)artz ‘bear’. This is the most common surname in Spain and also one of the most common surnames elsewhere in Spanish-speaking world. In the US, where it is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos in NM), it is the sixth most frequent surname. Compare De Garcia .

variants.name: Grassia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Amedeo, Maurizio, Pasquale, Rocco, Salvatore.
Italian: from a metathesized form of the personal nameGarsia, from Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Arcia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Francisco, Arturo, Dagoberto, Enrique, Jose, Ramiro, Adalberto, Aleida, Alejandro, Alfredo, Amalia.
Hispanic: probably an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcie
meanings: Altered form, under the French influence in LA, of Spanish García (see Garcia ). This form of the surname of Spanish origin is also found in southern France.
Spanish (Ávila): variant of García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garcilazo
meanings: Hispanic (mainly Mexico and Argentina): altered form of Spanish (Valencia) Garcilaso, a compound of the personal name Garci (a short form of García, see Garcia ) and the nickname laso ‘weak, exhausted’.

variants.name: Gacia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garsia
meanings: Hispanic: altered form of   Garcia  .

variants.name: Gracia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Juan, Manuel, Francisco, Jesus, Luis, Guadalupe, Mario, Pedro, Javier, Jorge.

Spanish; Catalan (Gràcia): from a short form of the Christian religious epithet de la Gracia, meaning ‘of mercy’ and forming part of the Marian name Maria de la Gracia ‘(Holy) Mary of Grace’, from Spanish gracia, Catalan gràcia ‘grace, mercy’.
In some cases possibly also an altered form of Spanish García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: De Garcia
meanings: Spanish and Galician (De García); Portuguese: patronymic from Spanish and Galician García and Portuguese Garcia , with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Garces
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jorge, Carlos, Juan, Jose, Manuel, Cesar, Rafael, Luis, Fernando, Gustavo, Javier, Mario.  Portuguese Albeiro, Paulo.
Spanish; Catalan (Garcés); Portuguese (Garcês): patronymic from the personal name García (see Garcia ).

variants.name: Garzia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Alfredo, Ernestina, Fernando, Juan, Pedro, Ricardo.  Italian Angelo, Antonio, Ciro, Corrado.
Italianized form of Spanish   Garcia  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Toledo
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Jorge, Manuel, Ramon, Rafael, Juan, Antonio, Jesus, Miguel, Roberto, Carlos, Francisco.

Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from the city in central Spain, which was the capital of the Visigothic state between the 6th and 8th centuries. Its role declined for three centuries after the Muslim invasion of Spain, until it was taken as the capital of the kingdom of Castile between the 11th and 16th centuries. It was a major cultural and political center throughout the Middle Ages, and was also the home of an important Jewish community. The placename, first recorded in Latin as Toletum, is of obscure etymology, possibly connected with Toleto in Piedmont; Jewish tradition connects it with Hebrew toledot ‘generations’, but this is no more than folk etymology. Compare Toledano .

Jewish (Sephardic): also an adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the surname their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Toledano
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Shlomo, Igal, Moshe, Shimon, Ehud, Shmuel, Yehuda.  Spanish Alberto, Gonzalo, Alina, Carlos, Conrado, Elena, Enrique, Evelia, Luis, Manuel, Miguel, Ramona.  French Gabrielle, Michel.
Spanish and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name for someone from Toledo (see   Toledo  ), from an adjectival form of the placename.

nameMeanings.name: Juan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , now also used as a given name in English-speaking countries, in spite of the unfavourable associations with Don Juan, the heartless seducer of  Mozart's  opera Don Giovanni (  1788 ) and libertine hero of  Byron's  satirical epic (  1819–24 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Juan Garcia de Toledo was born in 1257, in Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, his father, García Ibáñez de Toledo, was 25 and his mother, María Álvarez, was 22. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Inés García de Toledo. He died on 14 October 1288, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 31.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Juan Garcia de Toledo est né en 1257, à Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, son père, García Ibáñez de Toledo, avait 25 ans et sa mère, María Álvarez, avait 22 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Inés García de Toledo. Il est décédé le 14 octobre 1288, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 31 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Juan Garcia de Toledo 1257 in Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater García Ibáñez de Toledo 25 und seine Mutter María Álvarez 22. Er hatte mit Inés García de Toledo mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 14. Oktober 1288 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Juan Garcia de Toledo è nato il nel 1257 nel Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, il suo padre, García Ibáñez de Toledo, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, María Álvarez, aveva 22 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Inés García de Toledo. Morì il 14 ottobre 1288, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna, all'età di 31 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Juan Garcia de Toledoが1257年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドトーレドで生まれた時，父García Ibáñez de Toledoは25歳，母María Álvarezは22歳であった。彼はInés García de Toledoとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1288年10月14日にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドで31歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Juan Garcia de Toledo(이)가 1257년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도 톨도에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 García Ibáñez de Toledo(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 María Álvarez(은)는 22세였다. 그는 Inés García de Toledo(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1288년 10월 14일 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 31세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Juan Garcia de Toledo nasceu em 1257, em Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, seu pai, García Ibáñez de Toledo, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, María Álvarez, tinha 22 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Inés García de Toledo. Ele faleceu em 14 de outubro de 1288, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 31 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Juan Garcia de Toledo родился в 1257 г. в местности толедо, толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания, его отцу по имени García Ibáñez de Toledo было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали María Álvarez, 22. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Inés García de Toledo. Он умер 14 октября 1288 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 31 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Juan Garcia de Toledo nació en 1257, en Toledo, Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, su papá, García Ibáñez de Toledo, tenía 25 años y su mamá, María Álvarez, tenía 22 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Inés García de Toledo. Murió el 14 de octubre de 1288, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 31 años.
